title: sambucus racemosa f. pubens
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. pubens, also known as the American red-berried elder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. Its berries, flowers, and bark possess medicinal properties, particularly in their anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are derived from bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa f. pubens include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce fever and inflammation, and exhibit antiviral activity against infections such as the flu and COVID-19. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used as a natural remedy to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
